text.title: AWS Service Selection (Compute, Database, Architecture Decisions) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 rules grounded in AWS WA + Graviton economics + real-world migration debt.

rule.statement: Compute MUST default to Graviton (arm64) unless a binary dep blocks it. x86 selection MUST carry an explicit rationale.
rule.rationale: Graviton is ~20% cheaper at same perf and faster for many workloads. The default should be on; x86 is the exception with reason.

rule.statement: Lambda MUST be chosen only when the handler completes in < 15 min. Longer-running tasks MUST use Fargate / EKS / Batch.
rule.rationale: Lambda hard limit is 15 min; choosing it for longer tasks requires workarounds (chaining, Step Functions) that lose simplicity.

rule.statement: DynamoDB MUST be chosen only when access pattern is key-value + known queries. Relational queries / complex joins MUST use Aurora Serverless v2 / RDS.
rule.rationale: DDB is a key-value store; trying to do relational queries with scans is slow + expensive. Aurora Serverless v2 scales similarly without losing SQL.

rule.statement: Decision record MUST evaluate the workload against all 6 WA pillars before sign-off.
rule.rationale: Skipping a pillar means the workload has not been reviewed against it. Sustainability is the most-skipped — and most defensible to add Graviton for.

rule.statement: Gap list MUST be produced + remediated (or accepted with sign-off) before first prod deployment.
rule.rationale: Post-launch gaps are 10-100x more expensive to fix. The gap list is the bridge between design and ops.

rule.statement: If the Applies If preconditions in AGENTS.md do not all hold, OR any Skip If trigger fires, the agent MUST skip this methodology and pick a different one — applying the rules here on a mismatched input produces wrong output.
rule.rationale: Methodology selection errors cascade. The cheapest place to abort is before any rule fires.
